items.description: This Order gives effect to proposals submitted by the Construction Industry Training Board to the Department for the Economy for the imposition of a further levy upon employers in the construction industry for the purpose of raising money towards the expenses of the Board.

items.title: The Seed Marketing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations, which apply in Northern Ireland, implement—

items.title: The Proceeds of Crime Act (Appeals under Part 4) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order amends the Proceeds of Crime Act (Appeals under Part 4) Order 2003 (S.I. 2003/458) (“the 2003 Order”) by inserting into Part 3 (appeal to Supreme Court) of the 2003 Order an appeal under section 163B of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (2002 c.29) (“the 2002 Act”). Part 4 of the 2002 Act provides for confiscation proceedings in Northern Ireland. Section 29 (orders for securing compliance with confiscation order) of the Serious Crime Act 2015 (2015 c.9) inserted into the 2002 Act new sections 163A and 163B. The new order introduced under section 163A is known as a “compliance order”. Section 163B(4) to (7) provides for the appeal of a compliance order to the Supreme Court. Section 29 came into operation on 1st June 2015.

items.title: The Justice (2015 Act) (Commencement No. 6) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order brings sections 77 to 81 of the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (c.9 (N.I.)) into operation on 1st July 2016.

items.title: The Justice (2016 Act) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order brings into operation certain provisions of the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 (c.21 (N.I.)) (“the Act”).

items.title: The Food Information (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations make provision to enforce the requirements of Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) No 828/2014 on the requirements for the provision of information to consumers on the absence or reduced presence of gluten in food. The Regulations are made having regard in particular to Article 36(3)(d) of Regulation (EU) No 1169/2011 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 25th October 2011 on the provision of food information to consumers (“FIC”) which were implemented by the Food Information Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2014 (“the 2014 Regulations).

items.title: The Pensions (2015 Act) (Consequential, Supplementary and Incidental Amendments) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: The Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 creates a new state pension for people reaching pensionable age on or after 6th April 2016. This Order makes consequential, supplementary and incidental amendments to provisions of secondary legislation.

items.title: The Welfare Supplementary Payment (Loss of Disability Living Allowance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations introduce welfare supplementary payments to mitigate impacts from implementation of the Welfare Reform (Northern Ireland) Order 2015 (“the Order”) as defined in the document “A Fresh Start the Stormont Agreement and Implementation Plan”.

items.title: The Food Safety (Information and Compositional Requirements) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations make provision to enforce Regulation (EU) No 609/2013 of the European Parliament and of the Council, on the provisions of food intended for infants and young children, food for special medical purposes, and total diet replacement for weight control and repealing Council Directive 92/52/EEC, Commission Directives 96/8/EC, 1999/21/EC, 2006/125/EC and 2006/141/EC, Directive 2009/39/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council and Commission Regulations (EC) No 41/2009 and (EC) No 953/2009.

items.title: The Renewables Obligation Closure (No.2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order makes provision for no new renewables obligation certificates (ROCs) to be issued under a renewables obligation order in respect of electricity generated by a small onshore wind generating station after 30thJune 2016 unless the electricity is generated in any one of the circumstances provided for in this Order.

items.title: The Welfare Supplementary Payment (Loss of Carer Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations introduce welfare supplementary payments to mitigate impacts from implementation of the Welfare reform (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2015 as defined in the document “A Fresh Start the Stormont Agreement and Implementation Plan”.

items.title: The Welfare Supplementary Payment (Loss of Disability-Related Premiums) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations introduce welfare supplementary payments to mitigate impacts from implementation of the Welfare Reform (Northern Ireland) Order 2015 as defined in the document “A Fresh Start the Stormont Agreement and Implementation Plan”.

items.title: The Registered Rents (Increase) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order increases the rents registered with the rent officer for Northern Ireland under Part IV of the Private Tenancies (Northern Ireland) Order 2006 during the period beginning on 2nd April 2007 and ending on 3rd April 2016 by 1.3 percent.

items.description: These Regulations amend the Landlord Registration Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2014 to require that information held by the registrar and not included in the register of landlords shall, on request from an authorised officer, be disclosed to the Department for Communities for certain purposes.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 (revoked)

items.title: The Rates (Small Business Hereditament Relief) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016

items.title: The Discretionary Support Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under articles 135 & 136 (5) of the Welfare Reform (Northern Ireland) Order 2015, provide for the establishment of a discretionary support fund to be administered by the Department, to provide emergency loans and grants to claimants who find themselves in extreme, exceptional or crisis circumstances which present a significant risk to the health, safety or well-being of the claimant or a member of the claimant's family.

items.title: The Crown Court (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2016

The Order requires district councils to notify the operator of the food business establishment and provide a sticker and information within 14 days.

items.title: The Hybrid Schemes Quality Requirements (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Rules amend the Hybrid Schemes Quality Requirements Rules (Northern Ireland) 2012, in consequence of the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (c. 5 (N.I.)), to reflect the introduction of the alternative tests of scheme quality for defined benefit and hybrid schemes and the abolition of contracting-out for salary related pension schemes.

items.title: The Employers’ Duties (Implementation) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend regulations 5 and 6 of the Employers’ Duties (Implementation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2010 (“the 2010 Regulations”).

items.title: The Environmental Better Regulation (2016 Act) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016

items.title: The Optical Charges and Payments (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016

items.title: The Civil Legal Services (Financial) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Civil Legal Services (Financial) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 by inserting new provisions to provide that civil legal services funding for representation under the Access to Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2003 is available without an assessment of an individual’s financial resources for proceedings under:

items.title: The Superannuation (Northern Ireland Public Services Ombudsman) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order adds employment by the Northern Ireland Public Services Ombudsman to the kinds of employment listed in Schedule 1 to the Superannuation (Northern Ireland) Order 1972, for which the Department of Finance may make pension schemes. It has retrospective effect from 1st April 2016 by virtue of Article 3(7) of the 1972 Order.

items.title: The Public Service (Civil Servants and Others) Pensions (Consequential Provisions) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: Chapter 3 of Part 4 of the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993 concerns “anti-franking”, or the protection of increases in guaranteed minimum pensions (“GMPs”) which contracted-out schemes had to provide until 1997. For this purpose, the Regulations provide that the “cessation date”, when a person ceases to be in contracted-out employment under the old scheme (and from which point GMPs may need to be increased) is treated as the date when the person leaves the new scheme, and not the date when they transfer from their old scheme to their new scheme. Regulation 9 of The Public Service (Civil Servants and Others) Pensions (Consequential Provisions) Regulations (NI) 2015 includes a modification of the definition of “cessation date” in section 83 of the 1993 Act; that definition was amended by paragraph 38 of Schedule 13 to the Pensions Act (NI) 2015 as part of the abolition of contracting-out. On the coming into operation of regulation 4 of these Regulations, regulation 9 of The Public Service (Civil Servants and Others) Pensions (Consequential Provisions) Regulations (NI) 2015 shall cease to have effect and regulation 9A shall have effect.

items.title: The Public Service Pensions (Northern Ireland Public Services Ombudsman) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016

items.description: These Regulations facilitate the implementation of Regulation (EU) No. 165/2014 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 4 February 2014 on tachographs in road transport, repealing Council Regulation (EEC) No. 3281/85 on recording equipment in road transport and amending Regulation (EC) No. 561/2006 of the European Parliament and of the Council on the harmonisation of certain social legislation relating to road transport.

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Service Vehicles Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1985 (“the 1985 Regulations”) to introduce new duties for drivers of regulated public service vehicles in relation to passengers who are disabled.

items.title: The Food Hygiene (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations make various amendments to the Food Safety and Hygiene Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 (S.R. 2006 No.3) to provide for the execution and enforcement in Northern Ireland of Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2015/1375 laying down specific rules on official controls for Trichinella in meat (OJ No. L212, 11.8.2015, p.7).

items.title: The Carlingford Area (Definition of the Mouth of the Clanrye River) Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations define the mouth of the Clanrye River.

items.title: The Bus Lanes (Andersonstown Road and Stewartstown Road Belfast – between Finaghy Road North and Michael Ferguson Roundabout) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Ballymena) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Roads (Speed Limit) (No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Salaries (Assembly Ombudsman and Commissioner for Complaints) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order provides for an increase in the annual salaries payable to the Assembly Ombudsman for Northern Ireland and the Northern Ireland Commissioner for Complaints.

items.title: The Motor Hackney Carriages (Bangor) Bye-Laws (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Bangor) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Parking Places (Disabled Persons’ Vehicles) (Amendment No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Pensions (2015 Act) (Consequential Amendments) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order makes amendments to the Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (“the Administration Act”) and the Social Security (Northern Ireland) Order 1998 (“the 1998 Order”) which are consequential on the introduction of State Pension for persons reaching pensionable age on or after 6 April 2016 by the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015.

items.title: The Student Fees (Amounts) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Student Fees (Amounts) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”) by increasing the basic and higher amounts prescribed in the 2005 Regulations. In each case the increases are by less than 3%, and will come into operation on 1st September 2017.

items.title: The Health and Personal Social Services (Quality, Improvement and Regulation) (2003 Order) (Commencement No.6 and Transitional Provisions) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 3 October 2016 further provisions of the Health and Personal Social Services (Quality, Improvement and Regulation) (Northern Ireland) Order 2003 (“the 2003 Order”) as set out in Article 2 to enable regulation of fostering agencies by the RQIA.

items.title: The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast) (Amendment No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Dungannon) (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Carrickfergus) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Rates (Increased Reduction for Recreational Hereditaments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: Article 31 of the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977 provides for a 80% reduction of rates on certain hereditaments, or parts of hereditaments, used solely for the purposes of certain recreations. These Regulations increase that reduction to 100% in a case where—

items.title: The College Street, Belfast (Stopping-Up) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order stops up the road described in the Schedule to motor vehicles at the line specified therein.

items.title: The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Londonderry) (Amendment No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast) (Amendment No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Waiting Restrictions (Dundonald) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Prohibition of U-Turn (A26 Lisnevenagh Road, Kells) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Gas (Designation of Pipelines) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: Article 8(1) of the Gas (Northern Ireland) Order 1996 (“the Gas Order”) authorises the holder of a licence issued under sub-paragraph (a) to participate in the conveyance of gas from one place to another.

items.title: The Remuneration of the Chairman of the Northern Ireland Medical and Dental Training Agency Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations provide for the chairman of the Northern Ireland Medical and Dental Training Agency to be a prescribed person for the purposes of Article 89(1) of the Health and Personal Social Services (Northern Ireland) Order 1972 (“the 1972 Order”).

items.title: The Making Available on the Market and Supervision of Transfers of Explosives Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Mossley) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Bus Lanes Order (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Insolvency (Northern Ireland) Order 1989 (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order amends the Insolvency (Northern Ireland) Order 1989. It increases the bankruptcy level. This is the minimum debt or minimum total debts due to a creditor before that creditor can petition for bankruptcy of the debtor. The increase applies only to petitions presented on or after the day on which this Order comes into operation.

items.title: The Employment Rights (Increase of Limits) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order increases, from 14th February 2016, the limits applying to certain awards of industrial tribunals, the Fair Employment Tribunal or Labour Relations Agency statutory arbitration, and other amounts payable under employment legislation, as specified in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Derriaghy Road, Lisburn (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The A1 Hillsborough Road (between Hillsborough Roundabout and Pantridge Link), Hillsborough (Stopping-Up) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Route F1403 Footpath between Ashdene Park, Taghnevan and Tullygally East Road, Craigavon (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Sexual Offences Act 2003 (Prescribed Police Stations) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 (revoked)
items.description: The Sexual Offences Act 2003 (“the 2003 Act”) imposes notification requirements on offenders convicted of certain sexual offences. These offenders are called  “relevant offenders”. The 2003 Act requires relevant offenders to notify certain personal information to the police, both at the outset and periodically thereafter. Persons subject to a Violent Offences Prevention Order (VOPO) provided for in the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 or a Slavery and Trafficking Prevention Order (STPO), provided for in the Human Trafficking and Exploitation (Criminal Justice and Support for Victims) Act (Northern Ireland) 2015, are also required to notify in the same way as a relevant offender of the 2003 Act. Provision for the STPO commenced on 1 April 2016, but provision for the VOPO is yet to commence.

items.title: The Welfare Reform and Work (Northern Ireland) Order 2016 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2016
items.description: This Order brings into force Article 5 (benefit cap) of the Welfare Reform and Work (Northern Ireland) Order 2016 for remaining purposes on 7th November 2016.

items.title: The Benefit Cap (Housing Benefit and Universal Credit) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Benefit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (S.R.2006 No.405) (“the Housing Benefit Regulations”), the Housing Benefit (Decisions and Appeals) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 (S.R. 2001 No. 213), the Discretionary Financial Assistance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 (S.R. 2001 No. 216), the Universal Credit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 (S.R. 2016 No. 216) (“the Universal Credit Regulations”) and the Universal Credit (Transitional Provisions) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 (S.R.2016 No. 226). These Regulations make amendments in connection with the Welfare Reform and Work (Northern Ireland) Order 2016 (S.I. 2016/999 (N.I. 1)) (“the 2016 Order”) and introduce a new exemption from the benefit cap.

items.title: The Social Security (Expenses of Paying Sums in Relation to Vehicle Hire) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: Section 13B of the Social Security Administration (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (1992 c.8 (N.I.)) allows for regulations enabling the Department for Communities (“the Department”) to recover the administrative expenses of making payments directly from certain benefits payable to persons with mobility problems, to providers of motor vehicles on lease or hire purchase terms to those persons.

items.title: The County Court (Amendment No. 2) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Rules amend the County Court Rules (Northern Ireland) 1981 (S.R. 1981 No. 225) to make provision relating to an application for the grant of a liquor licence for an outdoor stadium.

items.title: The Richmond Crescent, Bangor (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Belfast Road, Carrickfergus (Part-Time 20mph Speed Limit) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Green Road, Conlig (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Cycle Routes (Amendment No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Newtownabbey) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Church Street / Mullagh Road, Maghera (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Baronscourt Lane, Carryduff (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Health and Personal Social Services (Superannuation), Health and Social Care (Pension Schemes) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Health and Personal Social Services (Superannuation) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1995 (S.R. 1995 No.95) (“the 1995 Regulations”), the Health and Social Care (Pension Scheme) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (S.R. 2008 No.256) (“the 2008 Regulations”) and the Health and Social Care Pension Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (S.R. 2015 No.120) (“the 2015 Regulations”).

items.title: The Attorney General’s Human Rights Guidance (Cooperation and Operational Independence) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order brings into operation guidance by the Attorney General for Northern Ireland, pursuant to section 8 of the Justice (Northern Ireland) Act 2004, which was laid before the Assembly on 5th October 2016.

items.title: The Justice (2015 Act) (Commencement No. 7 and Saving Provisions) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order brings the provisions of the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (c.9 (N.I.)) (“the Act”) specified in Article 2 into operation on 31st October 2016.

items.title: The Rates (Amendment) (2016 Act) (Commencement No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of section 1 of the Rates (Amendment) Act (Northern Ireland) 2016. Section 1 was partially commenced for regulation-making purposes by the Rates (Amendment) (2016 Act) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016 (S.R. 2016 No. 331 (C. 25)). This Order now commences that section for all other purposes.

items.title: The Welfare Supplementary Payment (Benefit Cap) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Welfare Supplementary Payments Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 (“The Welfare Supplementary Payment Regulations”) which were introduced to mitigate impacts from the implementation of the Welfare Reform (Northern Ireland) Regulations 2015 as defined in the document “A Fresh Start the Stormont Agreement and the Implementation Plan”.

items.title: The Moyallan Road, Portadown and College Lands Road, Charlemont (Part-Time 20mph Speed Limit) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Child Support (Deduction from Earnings Orders, Amendment and Modification and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend and modify the Child Support (Collection and Enforcement) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1992 (S.R. 1992 No. 390) (“the 1992 Regulations”). They amend the Child Support (Meaning of Child and New Calculation Rules) (Consequential and Miscellaneous Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2012 (S.R. 2012 No. 438) (“the 2012 Regulations”) and the Child Support (Ending Liability in Existing Cases and Transition to New Calculation Rules) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2014 (S.R. 2014 No. 191) (“the Ending Liability Regulations”). These Regulations also dis-apply the provisions in section 29(4) to (7) of the Child Support Order (Northern Ireland) 1991 (S.I. 1991/2628 (N.I. 23)) in relation to a segment 5 case where liability is ended whilst these Regulations are in operation. A segment 5 case is one within the meaning given in the scheme prepared by the Department under the Ending Liability Regulations (“the Ending Liability Scheme”).

items.title: The Further Education (Governing Body of Institution of Further Education) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Further Education (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 to remove the requirement on the Department for the Economy to appoint two persons nominated by the Education Authority (formerly the Education and Library Boards) to each of the Governing Bodies of the Northern Ireland Further Education Institutions. In addition, they allow for existing Education Authority appointments to continue within the confines of their terms of appointment.

items.title: The Groganstown Road, Belfast (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Social Security (Great Britain Reciprocal Arrangements) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations give effect in Northern Ireland to reciprocal arrangements between Great Britain and Northern Ireland relating to social security. The arrangements are made between the Minister of State for Employment on behalf of the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, with the consent of the Treasury, of the one part and the Minister for Communities in Northern Ireland, with the consent of the Department of Finance, of the other part. These arrangements are contained in the exchange of letters set out in the Schedule to these Regulations.

items.title: The Public Use of the Records (Management and Fees) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Rules revoke and replace the Public Use of the Records (Management and Fees) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2009.

items.title: The Energy Performance of Buildings (Certificates and Inspections) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Energy Performance of Buildings (Certificates and Inspections) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations implement in part, Directive 2010/31/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council of 19 May 2010 on the energy performance of buildings (recast)(“the Directive”).

items.title: The Civil Legal Services (Remuneration) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order amends the Civil Legal Services (Remuneration) Order (Northern Ireland) 2015 by inserting new provisions to provide that remuneration is available under civil legal services for representation in those proceedings referred to in Articles 3.

items.title: The Legal Aid for Crown Court Proceedings (Costs) (Amendment No. 3) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Rules amend the Legal Aid for Crown Court Proceedings (Costs) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2005, which prescribe the remuneration for solicitors and counsel assigned under Articles 29 or 36(2) of the Legal Aid, Advice and Assistance (Northern Ireland) Order 1981.

items.title: The Magistrates’ Courts and County Court Appeals (Criminal Legal Aid) (Costs) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates’ Courts and County Court Appeals (Criminal Legal Aid) (Costs) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2009, which prescribe the remuneration for solicitors and counsel assigned under Article 28, 28A or 30 of the Legal Aid, Advice and Assistance (Northern Ireland) Order 1981.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Amendment) (2016 Act) (Commencement No. 1) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 25th November 2016 the provisions of the Road Traffic (Amendment) Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 specified in the Schedule.

items.title: The Animal Feed (Composition, Marketing and Use) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations, which apply in relation to Northern Ireland only —

items.title: The Former Route C646 Killyliss Road, Dungannon (Abandonment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Juries Regulations (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order amends the Juries Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 (S.R. 1996 No.269) (“the 1996 Regulations”) in consequence of the provisions in Part 1 of the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (“the Act”) which create a single jurisdiction for county courts and magistrates’ courts, and provide for Northern Ireland to be divided into administrative court divisions by direction of the Department of Justice.

items.title: The Waste (Fees and Charges) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: The Department is required to have in place a charging scheme to ensure that, so far as practicable, fees and charges payable for waste management licences under the Waste and Contaminated Land (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 (the “1997 Order”) are sufficient to recover the costs for the Department in exercising its functions under the 1997 Order. However, fees for considering applications to register an undertaking carrying on an exempt activity and charges for considering applications for registration as carriers of, brokers of, or dealers in controlled waste are set out in legislation, as opposed to the charging scheme. The purpose of these Regulations is to increase those legislative fees and charges.

items.title: The Level Crossing (Bellarena) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order provides for the provision and maintenance of manually controlled barriers supervised by closed circuit television at Bellarena Level Crossing. Section 47 of the Railways Clauses Consolidation Act 1845 (which requires the railway undertaking to provide gates and gate-keepers), section 6 of the Railways Clauses Act 1863 (requirements as to lodges, etc.) and any other statutory provision imposing requirements to the same or similar effect, shall not apply to the crossing whilst this Order remains in force.

items.title: The Level Crossing (Castlerock) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order provides for the provision and maintenance of manually controlled barriers supervised by closed circuit television at Castlerock Level Crossing. Section 47 of the Railways Clauses Consolidation Act 1845 (which requires the railway undertaking to provide gates and gate-keepers), section 6 of the Railways Clauses Act 1863 (requirements as to lodges, etc.) and any other statutory provision imposing requirements to the same or similar effect, shall not apply to the crossing whilst this Order remains in force.

items.title: The Level Crossing (Coleraine (Bushmills Road)) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order provides for the provision and maintenance of manually controlled barriers supervised by closed circuit television at Coleraine (Bushmills Road) Level Crossing. Section 47 of the Railways Clauses Consolidation Act 1845 (which requires the railway undertaking to provide gates and gate-keepers), section 6 of the Railways Clauses Act 1863 (requirements as to lodges, etc.) and any other statutory provision imposing requirements to the same or similar effect, shall not apply to the crossing whilst this Order remains in force.

items.title: The Magistrates’ Courts (Licensing) (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates' Courts (Licensing) Rules (Northern Ireland) 1997 (S.R. 1997 No. 62) in consequence of the Licensing Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 (c. 24) which adds an outdoor stadium to the types of premises for which a court may grant a licence for the retail sale of intoxicating liquor. In particular, to prescribe the procedure for an application to attach, vary or remove conditions in respect of a licence for an outdoor stadium.

items.title: The Offshore Installations (Offshore Safety Directive) (Safety Case etc.) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Human Medicines (Amendment) Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Human Medicines Regulations 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”).

items.title: The Rathlin Island (Prohibited Methods of Fishing) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations prohibit fishing for sea-fish by mobile fishing gear in the waters within the Rathlin Zone. The boundaries of the Rathlin Zone correspond to the seaward boundaries of the Rathlin Island Special Area of Conservation, designated pursuant to Article 4 of Council Directive 92/43/EEC on the conservation of natural habitats and of wild fauna and flora (OJ L 206, 22.7.1992, p.7).

items.title: The Social Security (Credits, and Crediting and Treatment of Contributions) (Consequential and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to various sets of Social Security Regulations consequential on the changes made by the National Insurance Contributions Act 2015 (c. 5) to reform the payment and collection of Class 2 contributions and the introduction of accelerated payment of contributions by that Act. These Regulations also make various tidying-up miscellaneous amendments.

items.title: The Police (Conduct) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations apply where an allegation comes to the attention of an appropriate authority (as defined in regulation 3) which indicates that the conduct of a member may amount to misconduct or gross misconduct (as defined in regulation 3). This includes an allegation contained within a complaint by a member of the public or a matter referred to the Police Ombudsman for Northern Ireland (“the Ombudsman”), by the Northern Ireland Policing Board, the Department of Justice or the Secretary of State or investigated by the Ombudsman of his own motion.

items.title: The Human Trafficking and Exploitation (Criminal Justice and Support for Victims) (Independent Guardian) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations, which are made under section 21(5) of the Human Trafficking and Exploitation (Criminal Justice and Support for Victims) Act (Northern Ireland) 2015, make provision for the first time for—

items.title: The Welfare Reform (Northern Ireland) Order 2015 (Commencement No. 6 and Saving Provision) Order 2016
items.description: This Order brings into force provisions of the Welfare Reform (Northern Ireland) Order 2015 (S.I. 2015/2006 (N.I. 1)) which repeal or amend legislation relating to the social fund and the Office of the Social Fund Commissioner.

items.title: The Building (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Building Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2012 (as amended) (the principal Regulations) and they come into operation on 28th November 2016. The regulations transpose Article 8 and provide definitions from Article 2 of Directive 2014/61/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council of 15 May 2014 on measures to reduce the cost of deploying high-speed electronic communications networks. These regulations also clarify Articles 3, 4 and 9 of Directive 2010/31/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council of 19 May 2010 on the energy performance of buildings (recast).

items.title: The Police Service of Northern Ireland (Promotion) (Amendment) Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Service of Northern Ireland (Promotion) Regulations 2008 (“the 2008 Regulations”) to introduce the Constable to Inspector Fast Track Programme (“the Programme”). The amendments to regulation 4 of the 2008 Regulations have the effect of permitting constables and sergeants participating in the Programme to be promoted more quickly than if they were not participating in the Programme. The Schedule to the 2008 Regulations is also amended to permit constables and sergeants participating in the programme to take their qualifying assessments for promotion earlier than if they were not participating in the Programme.

items.title: The Motor Vehicles (Wearing of Seat Belts) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 (revoked)
items.description: Regulation 5 of the Motor Vehicles (Wearing of Seat Belts) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1993 (S.R. 1993 No. 362) (“the 1993 Regulations”) requires a person driving or riding in a motor vehicle who is aged 14 years and over to wear an adult seat belt. This requirement is subject to a number of exceptions.

items.title: The Caseins and Caseinates Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations will revoke and replace the Caseins and Caseinates Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1986 (S.R. 1986 No. 40) and the Caseins and Caseinates (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1990 (S.R. 1990 No. 37).

items.title: The Civil Legal Services (Scope) (No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend Schedule 2 to the Access to Justice (Northern Ireland) Order 2003 regarding the proceedings which may be funded as civil legal services.

items.title: The Justice (2015 Act) (Commencement No.8) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order brings into operation provision made in Part 8 of the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (c.9 (N.I.)) (“the Act”) for the Violent Offences Prevention Order (VOPO), and as listed in Article 2 of this Order, to the extent that they are not already in force. The provisions of this Order come into operation on 1st December 2016.

items.title: The Insolvency (Monetary Limits) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order makes amendments to the Insolvency (Monetary Limits) Order (Northern Ireland) 1991 (“the Principal Order”).

items.title: The State Pension Revaluation for Transitional Pensions Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2016/1141) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under section 148AC of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 (c. 5), specifies 1 per cent as the percentage to be used as the revaluing percentage for a “protected payment” under the new state pension. This is the first time the power to make the Order has been used.

items.title: The Police (Performance and Attendance) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations establish procedures for proceedings in respect of unsatisfactory performance or attendance of members of the rank of chief superintendent or below (excluding probationers).

items.title: The Railways Infrastructure (Access, Management and Licensing of Railway Undertakings) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations implement Directive 2012/34/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council of 21 November 2012, establishing a single European railway area (Recast) (O.J. No. L343, 14.12.12, p.32) (“the Directive”). The Directive repeals 3 key EU measures: (i) Council Directive 91/440/EEC of 29 July 1991 on the development of the communities railways (O.J. No. L237, 24.8.1991, p.25): (ii) Council Directive 95/18/EC of 19 June 1995 on the licensing of railway undertakings (O.J. No. L143, 27.06.1995, p.70): and (iii) Directive 2001/14/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council of 26 February 2001 on the allocation of railway infrastructure capacity and the levying of charges for the use of railway infrastructure and safety certification (O.J. No. L75, 5/03/2001, p.29), together with the various Directives amending these and consolidates these provisions in one place. The Directive also makes some substantive changes to the consolidation legal obligations, although most of these remain unchanged.

items.title: The Parking Places (Disabled Persons’ Vehicles) (Amendment No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Water Meters Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: Regulation 2(a) suspends the requirement, as stated in Article 81 of the Water and Sewerage Services (Northern Ireland) Order 2006 (the Order), for Northern Ireland Water to install water meters at all new domestic water connections.

items.title: The Occupational Pensions (Revaluation) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order, which corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2016/1102) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under paragraph 2(1) of Schedule 3 to the Pension Schemes Act 1993, specifies appropriate revaluation percentages. The percentages specified are relevant to the revaluation of benefits under occupational pension schemes, as required by section 80 of, and Schedule 2 to, the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993. The lower revaluation percentage introduced by the Pensions (No. 2) Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (c. 13 (N.I.)) does not apply to pensionable service before 6th April 2009.

items.title: The Justice (2011 Act) (Commencement No.9) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: Part 1, Chapter 1 of the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 (c.24) (“the 2011 Act”) provides for the offender levy (“the levy”). The levy involves the imposition of a financial amount by the court on specified court disposals, or the attachment of a financial amount on certain fixed penalties issued by the police or a vehicle examiner. Some provisions for the levy came into operation on 6th June 2012.

items.title: The Food Hygiene Rating (Fee and Fixed Penalty Amount) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order prescribes the fee to be charged for a review of a food hygiene rating (a re-rating).

items.title: The Court of Judicature Fees (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order amends the Court of Judicature Fees Order (Northern Ireland) 1996 (S.R. 1996 No. 100) to introduce new opening, annual administration and closing fees which, if the High Court or county court so orders, may be taken by the Accountant General from a fund in court held for the benefit of a minor or patient.

items.title: The Mines Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: The Mines Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 consolidate and modernise the law on health and safety in mines in Northern Ireland. The Regulations implement, as regards Northern Ireland, Council Directive 1992/104/EEC on the minimum requirements for improving the safety and health protection of workers in surface and underground mineral-extracting industries.

items.title: The Waiting Restrictions (Ballynure) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Roads (Speed Limit) (No. 4) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Police Appeals Tribunals Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations set out the circumstances in which a member of the Police Service of Northern Ireland (PSNI) may appeal to a police appeals tribunal. They also set out the procedures governing such an appeal.

items.title: The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast) (Amendment No. 5) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Victim Statement Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: Sections 33 to 35 of the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 make provision for a victim to be afforded an opportunity to make a written victim statement. This includes a family member where a person cannot act on their own behalf or where the victim has died. An opportunity to make a written supplementary statement may also be afforded on request.

items.title: The Social Security (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: Regulation 2 substitutes a new version of regulation 2(1) of the Discretionary Financial Assistance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 (S.R. 2001 No. 216). The substituted regulation clarifies the structure of the previous regulation and specifies that all tenants, regardless of tenure type, may be eligible for a discretionary housing payment if the amount of housing benefit which they are entitled to is less than it would have been, due to the removal of the family premium from the Housing Benefit applicable amount, and they are deemed to require additional financial assistance in order to meet housing costs. As a result of these changes, the definition of “registered housing association” in regulation 1(2) is no longer required and is therefore omitted.

items.title: The Bus Lanes Orders (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Belfast) (Amendment No. 6) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Waiting Restrictions (Saintfield) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Justice (2015 Act) (Commencement No.9) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order brings section 30 of the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (c.9) into operation on 30th December 2016.

items.title: The Social Security (Housing Costs Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987, the Jobseeker’s Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996 and the Employment and Support Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 to increase the waiting period before housing costs (including help with mortgage interest) can be met to 39 weeks from 13 weeks (regulations 2 to 4). This is subject to savings for certain claimants (regulation 6).

items.title: The Prohibition of Right-Hand Turn (Portadown) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Welfare Reform (Northern Ireland) Order 2015 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2016
items.description: This Order brings into force provisions of the Welfare Reform (Northern Ireland) Order 2015 relating to, in particular:

items.title: The Renewable Heat Incentive Schemes (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”) and the Domestic Renewable Heat Incentive Scheme Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2014 (“the 2014 Regulations”). The 2012 Regulations established a renewable heat incentive scheme for the non-domestic sector and the 2014 Regulations a renewable heat incentive scheme for the domestic sector, under which owners of plants which generate heat from specified renewable sources and meet specified criteria may receive payments at prescribed tariffs for the heat used for eligible purposes.

items.title: The Modern Slavery Act 2015 (Duty to co-operate with Commissioner) (Northern Irish public authority) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: Section 43 of the Modern Slavery Act 2015 (“the Act”) empowers the Independent Anti-Slavery Commissioner to request a “specified public authority” to co-operate with the Commissioner in any way that the Commissioner considers necessary for the purposes of the Commissioner’s functions. Specified public authorities are listed in Schedule 3 to the Act.

items.title: The Working Time Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations, to the extent they are made under section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 (c. 68), implement Council Directive 2003/88/EC concerning certain aspects of the organisation of working time (O.J. L299, 18.11.2003, p. 9) (which consolidated Council Directive 93/104/EC (O.J. L307, 13.12.93, p. 18) as amended by Council Directive 2000/34/EC (O.J. L195, 01.08.2000, p. 41)) and provisions concerning working time in Council Directive 94/33/EC on the protection of young people at work (O.J. L216, 20.8.94, p. 12). The provisions in the latter Directive which are implemented relate only to adolescents (those who have not attained the age of 18 and are over compulsory school age). These Regulations consolidate and replace the provisions of the Working Time Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1998 and the various Statutory Rules which amended those Regulations from 1998 to 2009.

items.title: The Animal Feed (Hygiene, Sampling etc. and Enforcement) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations provide for the continuing execution and enforcement of Regulation (EC) No. 183/2005 of the European Parliament and of the Council laying down requirements for feed hygiene (OJ No. L25, 8.2.2005, p.1), (“Regulation 183/2005”) and Commission Regulation (EC) No. 152/2009 laying down the methods of sampling and analysis for the official control of feed (OJ No. L54, 26.2.2009, p.1), (“Regulation 152/2009”), and also make provision as to administration generally in relation to feed law, in particular so as to give effect to Regulation (EC) No. 882/2004 of the European Parliament and of the Council on official controls performed to ensure the verification of compliance with feed and food law, animal health and animal welfare rules (OJ No. L191, 28.5.2004, p.1), (“Regulation 882/2004”).

items.title: The Public Service Pensions Revaluation (Earnings) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: Section 9 of the Public Service Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2014 applies to public service pension schemes established under that Act which require a revaluation of pensionable earnings, or a portion of such earnings accrued as a pension, by reference to a change in prices, earnings or both.

items.title: The Parking Places, Loading Bays and Waiting Restrictions Amendments (Lisburn) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Parking Places, Loading Bays and Waiting Restrictions (Coleraine) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Omagh) (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Animals and Animal Products (Examination for Residues and Maximum Residue Limits) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations replace provisions formerly contained in the Animals and Animal Products (Examination for Residues and Maximum Residue Limits) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1998 and Amending Regulations. In addition regulations 9, 10, 15, 20, 21 22 and 29 have been amended to include unlicensed substances in order to correct a previous omission.

items.title: The Benefit Cap (Housing Benefit) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Benefit Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2006 (“the Housing Benefit Regulations”) to make provision for a cap on the total amount of welfare benefits to which a person is entitled. They also make consequential amendments to the Housing Benefit (Decisions and Appeals) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 (“the Decisions and Appeals Regulations”).

items.title: The Social Security (Information-sharing in relation to Welfare Services etc.) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the extent of information-sharing which is permitted under Articles 120 to 123 of the Welfare Reform (Northern Ireland) Order 2015 (“the 2015 Order”).

items.title: The Mental Health Review Tribunal (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Rules amend the Mental Health Review Tribunal (Northern Ireland) Rules 1986 (S.R. 1986 No. 193) so as to allow the Tribunal to extend or abridge the time appointed by the Rules for doing any act where in the interests of justice to do so.

items.title: The Criminal Justice Act 1988 (Reviews of Sentencing) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: Part 4 of the Criminal Justice Act 1988, as amended by the Justice (Northern Ireland) Act 2002, (c.26) empowers the Director of Public Prosecutions for Northern Ireland (“the DPP”), with the leave of the Court of Appeal, to refer certain cases to that Court where the DPP considers that the sentences imposed were unduly lenient.

items.title: The Agriculture (Student fees)(Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Agriculture (Student fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007.

items.title: The Housing Benefit (Executive Determinations) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing Benefit (Executive Determinations) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008 to make changes to the manner in which the local housing allowance is determined.

items.title: The Human Trafficking and Exploitation (2015 Act) (Commencement No.2) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: Section 11 of, and Schedule 3 to, the Human Trafficking and Exploitation (Criminal Justice and Support for Victims) Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (“the 2015 Act”) make provision for courts in Northern Ireland to impose slavery and trafficking prevention orders (STPOs) upon offenders convicted of an offence of slavery, servitude and forced or compulsory labour, or of human trafficking, either upon sentencing or following an application by the Police Service of Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Social Security (Penalty Notice) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Penalty Notice) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1997 to prescribe changes to the form of notice given to a person who is offered the option to pay an administrative penalty as an alternative to prosecution for a social security benefit offence.

items.title: The Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (Search, Seizure and Detention of Property: Code of Practice) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016 (revoked)
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 1stMarch2016 a code of practice made under section 195T(1) of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (“the Act”) in connection with—

items.title: The Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (Cash Searches: Code of Practice) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016 (revoked)
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 1stMarch2016 a code of practice made under section 293A(1) of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (“the 2002 Act”) in connection with the exercise of the powers conferred by virtue of section 289 of the 2002 Act.

items.title: The Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (Investigations: Code of Practice) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016 (revoked)
items.description: This Order brings into operation on 1stMarch 2016 a code of practice made under section 377ZA(1) of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (“the Act”). The code of practice relates to the exercise of functions under Chapter2 of Part8 of the Act by constables and accredited financial investigators in Northern Ireland.

items.title: The Social Security (Lone Parents and Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Income Support (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 (“the Income Support Regulations”), the Jobseekers Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1996, the Social Security (Work-focused Interviews for Lone Parents) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2001 (“the Lone Parents Regulations”), the Social Security (Work-focused Interviews) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 (“the Work-focused Interviews Regulations”) and the Employment and Support Allowance Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2008.

items.title: The Pensions (2015 Act) (Commencement No. 3) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of certain provisions of the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (“the Act”), for regulation making purposes only, on 25th February 2016.

items.title: The Rates (Automatic Telling Machines) (Designation of Rural Areas) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016 (revoked)
items.description: This Order sets out those local government wards which are designated as rural areas for the purposes of Article 42(1F) of the Rates (Northern Ireland) Order 1977 which exempts from rates any automatic telling machine which is situated in an area so designated.

items.title: The Parking and Waiting Restrictions (Lurgan) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Superannuation (Certification Officer for Northern Ireland) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order adds employment in the office of the Certification Officer for Northern Ireland to the kinds of employment listed in Schedule 1 to the Superannuation (Northern Ireland) Order 1972, for which the Department of Finance and Personnel may make pension schemes. It has retrospective effect from 15th June 2012 by virtue of Article 3(7) of the 1972 Order.

items.title: The Public Service Pensions (Certification Officer for Northern Ireland) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations specify persons employed in the office of Certification Officer for Northern Ireland as persons to whom the scheme, established under The Public Service (Civil Servants and Others) Pension Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2014, may potentially relate, in accordance with section 25(3) of the Public Service Pensions (Northern Ireland) Act 2014. It has retrospective effect from 15th June 2012 by virtue of section 3(3)(b) of the 2014 Act.

items.title: The Social Security Revaluation of Earnings Factors Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2016/205) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under section 148 of the Social Security Administration Act 1992. It directs that the earnings factors relevant to the calculation of the additional pension in the rate of any long-term benefit or of any guaranteed minimum pension, or to any other calculation required under Part III of the Pension Schemes (Northern Ireland) Act 1993 are to be increased for the tax years specified in the Schedule to this Order by the percentage of their amount specified in that Schedule. The percentages for the tax years from and including 2000 - 2001 are also relevant for the purpose of revaluing state scheme pension debits and credits in accordance with sections 45B(6), 55A(5) and 55B(6) of the Social Security Contributions and Benefits (Northern Ireland) Act 1992 (“the 1992 Act”). The percentage for the tax year 2015 - 2016 is 2 per cent, and those for earlier tax years have been increased so that the earnings factors for those years are revalued at 2015 - 2016 earning levels.

items.title: The Train Driving Licences and Certificates (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Train Driving Licences and Certificates Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2010, (S.R. 2010 No. 132), (“the 2010 Regulations”) in order to implement Commission Directive 2014/82/EU of 24th June 2014 amending Directive 2007/59/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council as regards general professional knowledge and medical and licence requirements (O.J No. L184, 25.6.14, p.11).

items.title: The Damages for Bereavement (Variation of Sum) (Northern Ireland) Order 2016
items.description: This Order, which applies only to causes of action accruing on or after 1st April 2016, amends Article 3A(3) of the Fatal Accidents (Northern Ireland) Order 1977 (S.I. 1977/1251 (N.I. 18)) increasing the sum which may be awarded for damages for bereavement in Northern Ireland from £11,800 to £14,200.

items.title: The Zoonoses (Fees) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Zoonoses (Fees) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2011.

items.title: The Departments (Transfer of Functions) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This order transfers certain functions of Northern Ireland departments to other Northern Ireland departments.

items.title: The Charities Act 2008 (Designated Religious Charities) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order specifies section 86 of the Charities Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 as a provision which does not apply to designated religious charities.

items.title: The Pensions (2015 Act) (Consequential and Supplementary Amendments) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order makes amendments to primary legislation which are consequential on or supplementary to the introduction of the new state pension for people reaching pensionable age on or after 6th April 2016 by the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015.

items.title: The Producer Responsibility Obligations (Packaging Waste) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Producer Responsibility Obligations (Packaging Waste) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2007(S.R. 2007 No. 198) (“the 2007 Regulations”). The 2007 Regulations impose on producers and schemes the obligation to recover and recycle packaging waste in order to attain the recovery and recycling targets set out in Article 6(1) of Council Directive 94/62/EC on packaging and packaging waste (OJ No. L 365, 31.12.1994, p.10), as last amended by Commission Directive 2013/2/EU (OJ No. L 37, 8.2.2013, p10).

items.title: Change of Council Name (Derry and Strabane City Council) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations change the name of the council for the local government district of Derry and Strabane to Derry City and Strabane.

items.title: The State Pension (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations introduce further detailed rules relating to the new state pension. The Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (“the Act”) creates the new state pension for people reaching pensionable age on or after 6th April 2016 (see Part 1 of that Act). It replaces the existing state pension for those people.

items.title: The Modern Slavery Act 2015 (Power of arrest: Code of Practice) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order brings into operation a Code of Practice to be followed by Northern Ireland constables and enforcement officers when arresting a person under the power conferred by paragraph 22(2) of Schedule 2 to the Modern Slavery Act 2015. The Code takes effect upon commencement of Part 3 of, and Schedule 2 to, the Modern Slavery Act 2015.

items.title: The Police Pensions (Additional Voluntary Contributions) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: The Royal Ulster Constabulary Pensions (Additional Voluntary Contributions) Regulations 1993 (“the principal regulations”) provides for an in-house Additional Voluntary Contribution (“the AVC”) scheme. The AVC scheme was administered by The Equitable Life Assurance Society from 5th April 1990 and latterly, since 1st December 2002, by The Standard Life Assurance Company. The Police Service of Northern Ireland AVC scheme closed to new members as of 1st October 2010.

items.title: The Road Races (Mid-Antrim 150) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Renewables Obligation (Amendment) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order amends the Renewables Obligation (Northern Ireland) Order 2009 (“the 2009 Order”). Most of the amendments relate to the use of biomass to generate electricity.

items.title: The Bus Lanes (Upper Newtownards Road, Belfast – between Holywood Road and Sandown Road) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Control of Traffic (A2, Shore Road, Greenisland) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
The Whole of Government Accounts (Designation of Bodies) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order designates bodies, for the financial year ending with 31st March 2016, for the purposes of section 15 of the Government Resources and Accounts Act (Northern Ireland) 2001. The Department of Finance and Personnel (“the Department”) intends to pass the information provided by bodies so designated in the Schedule to this Order to HM Treasury for use in the preparation of the Whole of Government Accounts (WGA). The designation enables the Department to require those bodies to provide financial information in relation to the year in question. The bodies designated include the District Councils for the 11 Local Government Districts established pursuant to the Local Government (Boundaries) Act (NorthernIreland) 2008.

items.title: The State Pension (Amendment No. 2) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations set the full rate of the new state pension by amending the State Pension Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2015 (S.R. 2015 No. 315). The Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (c. 5) creates the new state pension for people reaching pensionable age on or after 6th April 2016 (see Part 1 of that Act). It replaces the existing state pension for those people. The full rate is set at £155.65 per week.

items.title: The Departments (2016 Act) (Commencement) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order provides for the coming into operation of the following provisions of the Departments Act (Northern Ireland) 2016 —

items.title: Change of District Name (Derry and Strabane) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order changes the name of the local government district of Derry and Strabane identified in Article 2(1) of, and Schedule 1 to, the Local Government (Boundaries) Order (Northern Ireland) 2012 to Derry City and Strabane.

items.title: The Taxis Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (Retention and Disposal of Seized Motor Vehicles, Equipment and Items) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations provide for the retention, safe keeping, release and disposal, by the Department, police or authorised officers, of vehicles, equipment and items seized under sections 38 and 41 of the Taxis Act (Northern Ireland) 2008 (“the 2008 Act”).

items.title: The Social Security (Claims and Payments) (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016 (revoked)
items.description: Regulation 2 of these Regulations amends paragraph 6 of Schedule 8B to the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 1987 by decreasing from £0∙46 to £0∙44 the fee which qualifying lenders pay for the purpose of defraying administrative expenses incurred by the Department for Social Development in making payments in respect of mortgage interest direct to those lenders.

items.title: The Social Security Benefits Up-rating Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This Order corresponds to an Order (S.I. 2016/230) made by the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions under section 150A of the Social Security Administration Act 1992 and includes details of the sums mentioned in that section.

items.title: The Magistrates’ Courts (Amendment) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Rules amend the Magistrates’ Courts Rules (Northern Ireland) 1984 (S.R. 1984 No. 225) to —

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Violent Offences Prevention Orders) Rules (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Rules contain provisions relating to Part 8 of the Justice Act (Northern Ireland) 2015 (2015 c.9) which makes provision in relation to violent offences prevention orders.

items.title: The Waste (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972. They amend a number of pieces of Northern Ireland legislation to bring into effect the changes introduced by Commission Directive (EU) 2015/1127 of 10th July 2015 amending Annex II of Directive 2008/98EC of the European Parliament and the Council on waste. The purpose of Directive 2015/1127 is to apply a climate correction factor to the R1 formula on the recovery of energy from waste.

items.title: The Waste Management Licensing (Amendment) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Waste Management Licensing Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2003 (the 2003 Regulations).

items.title: The Tobacco Retailer (Registration and Display of Notices) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: Section 1 of the Tobacco Retailers Act (Northern Ireland) 2014 (“the Act”) requires a registration authority to keep a register of persons carrying on a tobacco business.

items.title: The Tobacco Retailer (Fixed Penalty) (General) Regulations (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: These Regulations provide the form of fixed penalty notice which may be issued in respect of particular offences as listed under section 12(1) of the Tobacco Retailers Act (Northern Ireland) 2014.

items.title: The Pensions (2015 Act) (Pension Sharing on Divorce etc.) (Transitional Provision) Order (Northern Ireland) 2016
items.description: This is the first transitional provision Order made in respect of Part 1 of the Pensions Act (Northern Ireland) 2015. It makes transitional provision in relation to the coming into operation on 6th April 2016 of sections 13 to 15 of, and Schedules 8 to 11 to, that Act. These provisions allow for the continuation of state pension sharing on divorce or nullity of a marriage or dissolution or annulment of a civil partnership for people who reach state pension age on or after 6th April 2016 and are entitled to the new state pension at the transitional rate. This Order also makes provision for existing legislation to distinguish between rights to an old state pension which are shareable and rights to a new state pension which are shareable.

items.title: The A84 Trunk Road (Balquhidder Station) (50mph Speed Limit) Order 2016
The Scottish Parliament Elections (Returning Officer Fees and Charges) Regulations 2016 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations provide for the total amounts that are recoverable by returning officers for their services and expenses in connection with the conduct of elections for membership of the Scottish Parliament.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 2016
items.description: This Act of Sederunt substitutes a new Table of Fees for the Table of Fees in Schedule 1 to the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) (No. 2) 2002.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 2016
items.description: This Act of Sederunt substitutes a new Table of Fees for the Table of Fees in Schedule 1 to the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) (No. 2) 2002.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session 1994 and Sheriff Court Rules Amendment) (Miscellaneous) 2016
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session 1994) 1994.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules 1996 Amendment) (Miscellaneous) 2016
The Common Agricultural Policy (Direct Payments etc.) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Common Agricultural Policy (Direct Payments etc.) (Scotland) Regulations 2015 (“the principal Regulations”), which make provision in Scotland for the administration of Regulation (EU) No 1307/2013 of the European Parliament and of the Council establishing rules for direct payments to farmers under support schemes within the framework of the common agricultural policy (OJ L 347, 20.12.2013, p.608) (“the Direct Payments Regulation”), and other associated EU Regulations as referred to in regulation 2(1) of the principal Regulations.

items.title: The M90/A90 Edinburgh - Fraserburgh Trunk Road (Forth Road Bridge) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) (No. 2) Order 2016
The M8/A8 Trunk Road (Chapelhall) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turn) Order 2016
The Welfare Funds (Scotland) Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to the operation of welfare funds to be maintained by every local authority under the Welfare Funds (Scotland) Act 2015. They come into force on 1st April 2016, at which point that Act and provisions under it will replace the non-statutory arrangements under which local authorities currently maintain welfare funds.

items.title: The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2016
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2016
The Serious Crime Act 2015 (Commencement No. 1 and Saving Provision) (Scotland) Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations are the first commencement regulations made by the Scottish Ministers under the Serious Crime Act 2015 (“the Act”).

items.title: The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2016
The A77 Trunk Road (Ballantrae and Girvan) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2016
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2016
The Non-Domestic Rate (Scotland) Order 2016
items.description: This Order prescribes a rate of 48.4 pence in the pound as the non-domestic rate to be levied throughout Scotland in respect of the financial year 2016-2017. A rate of 48 pence in the pound was the figure prescribed by the Scottish Ministers as the non-domestic rate to be levied throughout Scotland for the financial year 2015-2016 (S.S.I. 2015/47).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Levying) (Scotland) Regulations 2016 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the amount payable in certain circumstances as non-domestic rates in respect of non-domestic subjects in Scotland. The non-domestic rate for subjects not covered by these Regulations is fixed by Order made under the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1975. For the year 2016-2017 the rate is fixed by the Non-Domestic Rate (Scotland) Order 2016 (S.S.I. 2016/113).

items.title: The Conservation of Salmon (Scotland) Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations make provision to regulate the killing of salmon in specified areas. The Scottish Ministers must carry out, or arrange for the carrying out of, an assessment of the conservation status of salmon in those areas described in Schedule 1.

items.title: The Salmon Carcass Tagging (Scotland) Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations make provision for and in connection with tagging the carcasses of salmon within inland waters in Scotland. The duty to affix a tag to salmon carcasses does not apply to any salmon which is taken in fish farms or where the exemptions at section 27 of the Salmon and Freshwater Fisheries (Consolidation) (Scotland) Act 2003 apply. They do not apply where salmon is caught by rod and line and where the salmon is taken in the Lower Esk south of the medium filum under the authority of a licence issued by the Environment Agency.

items.title: The Tweed Regulation (Salmon Carcass Tagging) Order 2016
items.description: This Order makes provision for and in connection with tagging the carcasses of salmon within inland waters in Scotland. The duty to affix a tag to salmon carcasses does not apply to any salmon which is taken by rod and line, in fish farms or where the exemptions at article 48 of the Scotland Act (River Tweed) Order 2006 apply.

items.title: The Tweed Regulation (Salmon Conservation) Order 2016 (revoked)
items.description: This Order makes provision to regulate the taking of salmon in the Tweed district. The Tweed district is defined in article 2 of the Scotland Act 1998 (River Tweed) Order 2006 (“the 2006 Order”). This Order places the Scottish Ministers under a duty to make, or make arrangements for, an assessment of the conservation status of salmon in the areas within the district described in article 2(1). Article 4 provides that the Scottish Ministers may agree a conservation plan with the River Tweed Commission.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Enterprise Areas) (Scotland) Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations provide relief from business rates in specified enterprise areas.

items.title: The Sea Fish (Prohibited Methods of Fishing) (Firth of Clyde) Order 2016 (revoked)
items.description: This Order prohibits, subject to certain exceptions, all methods of fishing within specified areas of the Firth of Clyde from 14th February until 30th April, during both 2016 and 2017 (articles 1(3) and 3(1)). It is made in accordance with the procedure set out in Article 46 of Council Regulation (EC) No 850/98 for the conservation of fishery resources through technical measures for the protection of juveniles of marine organisms and it remains in force until the end of 30th April 2017 (article 1(2)).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Steel Sites) (Scotland) Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations provide 100% relief from business rates in respect of lands and heritages at the addresses specified in regulation 3(2) if they are used for the sole or main purpose of carrying on the manufacture of basic iron and steel and of ferro-alloys.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Renewable Energy Generation Relief) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Non-Domestic Rates (Renewable Energy Generation Relief) (Scotland) Regulations 2010 (“the 2010 Regulations”).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Telecommunication Installations) (Scotland) Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations provide 100% relief from business rates in respect of lands and heritages in a Mobile Masts Pilot Area if they are occupied by a tower or mast used for the monitoring, processing or transmission of communications or other signals for the provision of electronic communications services and a new entry is made in the valuation roll on or after 1st April 2016.

items.title: The Local Authority (Capital Finance and Accounting) (Scotland) Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations make provision with respect to the powers of local authorities to borrow money, to incur debt through borrowing by way of credit arrangements and to maintain loans funds. They are made under the Local Government etc. (Scotland) Act 1994 (“the principal Act”). They will replace much of the provision in relation to local authority borrowing that is contained in Schedule 3 to the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1975 (“the 1975 Act”).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Unoccupied Property) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the Non-Domestic Rating (Unoccupied Property) (Scotland) Regulations 1994 (“the 1994 Regulations”).

items.title: The Concession Contracts (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations correct drafting errors in the Concession Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2016.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2016
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning (General Permitted Development) (Scotland) Order 1992 (“the 1992 Order”). Article 3 substitutes class 6H of Part 1A of the Schedule to the 1992 Order to provide permitted development rights for the installation, alteration or replacement of an air source heat pump on or within the curtilage of a dwelling.

items.title: The National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Regulations 1998 (“the 1998 Regulations”) which provide for payments to be made by means of a voucher system in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: The Human Trafficking and Exploitation (Scotland) Act 2015 (Commencement No. 1 and Transitory Provisions) Regulations 2016
items.description: These Regulations bring into force the following provisions of the Human Trafficking and Exploitation (Scotland) Act 2015 (“the Act”) on 31st May 2016: Parts 1 and 3, section 8, section 9 (but only for the purpose of making regulations under section 9(2)(b)(i) and (8)), section 10, section 11 (but only for the purpose of making regulations under section 11(7) and (8)), section 12 (but only for the purpose of making regulations under section 12(5)), sections 32, 33, 34(1) and 35 to 37, section 38 (but only for the purpose of making regulations under section 38(3) and (4)), section 39, section 43 and paragraphs 1, 3 and 6 of the Schedule.
